{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Togo women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name               = Togo
| Badge              = 
| FIFA Trigramme     = TOG
| Nickname           = ''Les Éperviers'' <br /> (The Sparrowhawks)
| Association        = [[Togolese Football Federation|Fédération Togolaise de Football]] (FTF)
| Sub-confederation  = [[West African Football Union|WAFU]] (West Africa)
| Confederation      = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach              = [[Patrice Neveu]]
| Captain            = [[Djené]]
| Most caps          = [[Alaixys Romao]] (97)
| Top scorer         = [[Emmanuel Adebayor]] ([[List of international goals scored by Emmanuel Adebayor|32]])
| Home Stadium       = [[Stade de Kégué]]
| FIFA Rank          = {{FIFA World Rankings|TOG}}
| FIFA max           = 46
| FIFA max date      = August 2006
| FIFA min           = 136
| FIFA min date      = April 2021
| Elo Rank           = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Togo}}
| Elo max            = 56
| Elo max date       = November 2005, January 2006
| Elo min            = 128
| Elo min date       = 4 September 1994
| pattern_la1        = _togo2021h
| pattern_b1         = _togo2021h
| pattern_ra1        = _togo2021h
| pattern_so1        =
| leftarm1           = FFEE00
| body1              = FFEE00
| rightarm1          = FFEE00
| shorts1            = FFEE00
| socks1             = FFEE00
| pattern_la2        = _pumacup2122g
| pattern_b2         = _pumacup2122g
| pattern_ra2        = _pumacup2122g
| pattern_sh2        =
| pattern_so2        =
| leftarm2           = 009150
| body2              = 009150
| rightarm2          = 009150
| shorts2            = 009150
| socks2             = 009150
| First game         = {{flagicon|FRA}} '''French Togoland''' 1–1 {{fb-rt|Gold Coast|name=Gold Coast and Trans-Volta Togoland}}<br />([[French Togoland]]; 13 October 1956)
| Largest win        = {{fb|Togo}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|SWZ|name=Swaziland}} <br /> ([[Accra]], Ghana; 11 November 2008) <br /> {{fb|Togo}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|Mauritius}} <br /> ([[Lomé]], Togo; 12 November 2017)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|MAR}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|TOG}} <br /> (Morocco; 28 October 1979) <br /> {{fb|TUN}} 7–0 {{fb-rt|Togo}} <br /> ([[Tunis]], Tunisia; 7 January 2000)
| World cup apps     = 1
| World cup first    = 2006
| World cup best     = Group stage ([[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]])
| Regional name      = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps  = 8
| Regional cup first = [[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972]]
| Regional cup best  = Quarter-finals ([[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]])
}}
[[File:Togo-nationalmannschaft.jpg|thumb|250px|Members of the Togolese national football team before a warm-up match in Biberach/Riss a few days before the 2006 World Cup]]
The '''Togo national football team''' ([[French language|French]]: ''Équipe nationale de football du Togo'') represents [[Togo]] in international [[Association football|football]] and is controlled by the [[Togolese Football Federation]]. The national football team of Togo made their debut in the [[FIFA World Cup]] in 2006. Their team bus underwent [[Togo national football team attack|a fatal attack]] in [[Angola]] prior to the [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations]]. They withdrew and were subsequently banned from the following two tournaments by the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF). In 2013 for the first time in history, Togo reached the quarter-finals of the [[Africa Cup of Nations]]. The team represents both [[FIFA]] and the Confederation of African Football (CAF).

==History==
They made their first [[FIFA World Cup]] appearance in their history in [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]], having been coached throughout the qualifying campaign by [[Stephen Keshi]]; German coach [[Otto Pfister]] managed the team at the finals, despite having resigned three days before their first match over a players' bonuses dispute, only to be persuaded by the players to return. Prior to gaining independence in 1960, the team was known as '''French Togoland'''.

===2006 World Cup===
Togo lost their opening game of the World Cup, despite having taken the lead against [[South Korea national football team|South Korea]] through a goal by [[Mohamed Kader]]. In the second half, [[Jean-Paul Abalo]] was sent off after 55 minutes, and goals from [[Lee Chun-Soo]] and [[Ahn Jung-Hwan]] sealed a 2–1 defeat for Togo.

Togo's next opponents in Group G were [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]], with the match scheduled for the afternoon of 19 June. However, the Togo squad and manager Pfister threatened to refuse to fulfill the fixture and take [[strike action]]. The squad and manager had been quoted as requesting payments from the [[Togolese Football Federation]] for participating in the tournament of around [[euro|€]]155,000 ([[United States dollar|US$]]192,000) with added bonuses for victories or draws. [[FIFA]] negotiated with the squad and manager on 17 June, persuading them to travel to [[Dortmund]] in time to fulfill the fixture;<ref>{{cite web|url=http://home.skysports.com/worldcup/article.aspx?hlid=396042&CPID=4&clid=1223&lid=13&title=Togo+strike+averted |title=Sky Sports &#124; Football News |publisher=Home.skysports.com |access-date=2010-02-04 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070125020203/http://home.skysports.com/worldcup/article.aspx?HLID=396042 |archive-date=2007-01-25 }}</ref> goals from [[Alexander Frei]] and [[Tranquillo Barnetta]] resulted in a 2–0 defeat. FIFA subsequently imposed a [[Swiss franc|CHF]]100,000 fine on the Togolese federation for "behaviour unworthy of a participant in the World Cup."

Togo's final group game against [[France national football team|France]] ended in a 2–0 defeat.

===Sierra Leone air disaster===
{{main|2007 Paramount Airlines helicopter crash}}

After a [[2008 African Nations Cup]] qualifier away to [[Sierra Leone national football team|Sierra Leone]] on 3 June 2007, 20 members of a delegation of sports officials from [[Togo]], including Togolese Sports Minister [[Richard Attipoe]], were killed when their [[helicopter]] exploded and crashed at [[Lungi International Airport]]. No players of the Togo national team were among the victims. The Togo players and officials of the team had been waiting to take the next helicopter flight to the island on which the airport is located.

===2010 bus ambush and ban===
{{main|Togo national football team attack}}

On 8 January 2010, the Togo team bus was attacked by gunmen as it travelled to the [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations]] tournament, killing three and injuring several others. The separatist group [[Front for the Liberation of the Enclave of Cabinda]] (FLEC) claimed responsibility for the attack. Goalkeeper [[Kodjovi Obilalé]] was reported dead a day after the attack, though he actually survived.<ref>{{cite web |url= http://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/10012010/58/african-cup-nations-noconfusion-togo-death-toll.html |title= African Cup of Nations&nbsp;— NoConfusion over Togo death toll |date= 2010-01-09 |agency= Reuters |access-date= 2010-02-01 |archive-date= 2010-01-12 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100112092636/http://uk.eurosport.yahoo.com/10012010/58/african-cup-nations-noconfusion-togo-death-toll.html |url-status= live }}</ref> Such reports were later dismissed by his club [[GSI Pontivy]] in a press announcement, stating the player was actually undergoing surgery in South Africa.<ref>{{cite web |title=Kodjovi Obilalé n'est pas décédé des suites de ses blessures (Agence AFP) |url=https://www.pontivy-football-gsi.com/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20070222032718/http://www.pontivy-football-gsi.com/ |archive-date=2007-02-22 |access-date=2010-02-01 |language=fr}}</ref>

Following the bus ambush attack, the [[Togolese Football Federation]] stated that they would withdraw from the [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations]]; despite claims that the team had since reversed the decision and would compete "to show our national colours, our values and that we are men" (as announced by [[Thomas Dossevi]]),<ref>{{cite web |url=http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-world/togo-in-dramatic-african-nations-cup-uturn-20100110-m0fu.html |title=Togo in dramatic African Nations Cup u-turn |work=Sydney Morning Herald |author=Nick Reeves |date=2010-01-10 |access-date=2010-02-01 |archive-date=2011-11-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111119125213/http://news.smh.com.au/breaking-news-world/togo-in-dramatic-african-nations-cup-uturn-20100110-m0fu.html |url-status=live }}</ref> the government later ordered that the team return home.<ref>{{cite news |title= Togo officially disqualified from Africa Cup of Nations |work= BBC Sport |publisher= BBC |url= https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8450529.stm |date= 2010-01-11 |access-date= 2010-02-01 |archive-date= 2010-01-14 |archive-url= https://web.archive.org/web/20100114071258/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/football/africa/8450529.stm |url-status= live }}</ref>

Following the team's withdrawal, The [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF) banned Togo from participating in the next two editions of the Cup of Nations and fined them US$50,000 because of the "decision taken by the political authorities".<ref name="CAF announcement">{{cite news | title = Togo's withdrawal | url = http://www.cafonline.com/competition/african-cup-of-nations-angola_2010/news/4587-togos-withdrawal.html | publisher = Confederation of African Football | date = 30 January 2010 | access-date = 31 January 2010 | archive-date = 2 April 2012 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20120402064158/http://www.cafonline.com/competition/african-cup-of-nations-angola_2010/news/4587-togos-withdrawal.html | url-status = live }}</ref><ref name="Togo banned from next two Africa Cups of Nations">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/football/africa/8489127.stm|title=Togo banned from next two Africa Cups of Nations|work=BBC Sport|publisher=BBC|access-date=30 January 2010|date=30 January 2010|archive-date=14 April 2012|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120414025534/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8489127.stm|url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Togo suspended for next two Africa Nations Cup">{{cite web|url=http://news.xinhuanet.com/english2010/sports/2010-01/31/c_13157462.htm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110609161553/http://news.xinhuanet.com/english2010/sports/2010-01/31/c_13157462.htm|url-status=dead|archive-date=June 9, 2011|title=Togo suspended for next two Africa Nations Cup|publisher=Xinhua|access-date=31 January 2010}}</ref> The CAF executive Committee considered that the Togolese team was in "forfeit notified less than twenty days before the start or during the final competition" (Art.&nbsp;78 of the Regulations for the Africa Cup of Nations),<ref name="CAF announcement"/><ref>{{cite book | title = Regulations of the Orange Africa Cup of Nations | url = http://www.cafonline.com/userfiles/file/CAN2010Eng.pdf | publisher = Confederation of African Football | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20100215030437/http://www.cafonline.com/userfiles/file/CAN2010Eng.pdf | archive-date = 15 February 2010 | url-status = dead }}</ref> rather than having withdrawn (Art.&nbsp;80), and refused to consider the circumstances as ''[[force majeure]]'' (Art.&nbsp;87). Togo's government immediately said they would sue as CAF "have no consideration for the lives of other human beings" and this is further "insulting to the family of those who lost their lives and those traumatized because of the attack".<ref name="Togo banned from next two Africa Cups of Nations"/> [[FIFA]] has yet to comment on the issue.<ref name="Togo banned from next two Africa Cups of Nations"/> Togo footballer [[Thomas Dossevi]] said, "We are a group of footballers who came under fire and now we can't play football anymore. They are crushing us".<ref name="Togo banned from next two Africa Cups of Nations"/> Togolese captain [[Emmanuel Adebayor]] described the decision as "outrageous" and said that CAF President [[Issa Hayatou]] had "completely betrayed" the Togo squad.<ref>{{cite news | title = Togo captain Emmanuel Adebayor slams 'outrageous' ban | url = https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8489883.stm | publisher = BBC Sport | date = 31 January 2010 | access-date = 31 January 2010 | archive-date = 1 February 2010 | archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20100201053422/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/football/africa/8489883.stm | url-status = live }}</ref> FIFA subsequently helped lifted their ban entirely.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2010-05-08 |title=FIFA boss helps lift African Cup ban on Togo |url=https://www.france24.com/en/20100508-fifa-sepp-blatter-lifts-african-nations-cup-ban-togo |access-date=2024-08-18 |website=France 24 |language=en}}</ref>

As a result of the events, Emmanuel Adebayor announced his retirement from international football on 12 April 2010.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Taylor |first=Daniel |date=2010-04-12 |title=Emmanuel Adebayor retires from international football after bus attack |url=https://www.theguardian.com/football/2010/apr/12/emmanuel-adebayor-international-football |access-date=2024-08-18 |work=The Guardian |language=en-GB |issn=0261-3077}}</ref> But on 22 March 2011, Adebayor announced that he was again available for the national team.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Adebayor returns for Togo |url=https://sports.ndtv.com/football/adebayor-returns-for-togo-1573631 |access-date=2024-08-18 |website=NDTVSports.com |language=en}}</ref>

===Fake Togo team===
On 7 September 2010, Togo allegedly played Bahrain in a friendly losing the match 3–0. However, on 14 September, the Togo FA claimed that a fake team had played against Bahrain. Togo's Sports Minister Christophe Tchao said to the ''Jeune Afrique'' magazine that nobody in Togo had "ever been informed of such a game".<ref>{{cite news| title = 'Fake' Togo football team at Bahrain match being investigated| url = https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-11304208| publisher = BBC News| date = 15 September 2010| access-date = 20 June 2018| archive-date = 3 December 2017| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20171203174831/http://www.bbc.co.uk/news/world-middle-east-11304208| url-status = live}}</ref> On 20 September 2010, it was revealed that former Togo manager [[Bana Tchanilé]] was the culprit and the Togo FA have given him a three-year ban in addition to the two-year ban he got in July 2010 for taking Togo players to play a tournament in Egypt.<ref>{{cite web |title=Fake mastermind behind fake Togo team revealed! |url=https://sports.yahoo.com/soccer/blog/dirty-tackle/post/Fake-mastermind-behind-fake-Togo-team-revealed-?urn=sow-271032 |publisher=Yahoo |date=20 September 2010 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100926003305/http://sports.yahoo.com/soccer/blog/dirty-tackle/post/Fake-mastermind-behind-fake-Togo-team-revealed-?urn=sow-271032 |archive-date=26 September 2010 }}</ref> The match fixing has been linked to [[Wilson Raj Perumal]] and the [[Singapore]]an [[match-fixing]] [[syndicate]] allegedly run by [[Tan Seet Eng]].<ref name="Indepenent">{{cite web | url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/asia/dan-tan-the-man-who-fixed-football-8554751.html |archive-url=https://ghostarchive.org/archive/20220621/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/world/asia/dan-tan-the-man-who-fixed-football-8554751.html |archive-date=2022-06-21 |url-access=subscription |url-status=live | title=Dan Tan: the man who fixed football | work=The Independent | date=29 March 2013 | access-date=30 November 2013|author=Buncombe, Andrew}}</ref>

===2014 World Cup Qualification===
Togo began qualification for the 2014 World Cup on 11 November 2011, against Guinea-Bissau. They drew in the first leg 1–1. On 15 November 2011, they won the return leg 1–0. On 3 June 2012, they played Libya in Lome and drew 1–1. Shortly after on 10 June they played Congo DR at Kinshasa and lost 2–0. They resumed on 3 March 2013, and played Cameroon in Yaounde and lost 2–1. They met again on 9 June in Lome and Togo won 2–0. In the end, Togo failed to qualify for the 2014 World Cup in Brazil.

==Results and fixtures==
The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

===2025===
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 5 September
|time       = {{UTZ|19:00|1}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|MTN}}
|score      = 2–0
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020776
|team2      = {{fb|TOG}}
|goals1     =
*[[Papa Ndiaga Yade|Yade]] {{goal|14}}
*[[Aly Abeid|Abeid]] {{goal|69}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Nouadhibou Municipal Stadium]]
|location   = [[Nouadhibou]], Mauritania
|attendance =
|referee    = Lucky Kasalirwe ([[Federation of Uganda Football Associations|Uganda]])
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 9 September
|time       = {{UTZ|16:00|0}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score      = 1–0
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400020774
|team2      = {{fb|SDN}}
|goals1     =
*[[Sadik Fofana|Fofana]] {{goal|6}}
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Stade de Kégué]]
|location   = [[Lomé]], Togo
|attendance =
|referee    = Andofetra Rakotojaona ([[Malagasy Football Federation|Madagascar]])
|result     = W
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 10 October
|time       = 14:00
|team1      = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score      = 0–1
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021039
|team2      = {{fb|COD}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
*[[Cedric Bakambu|Bakambu]] {{goal|7}}
|stadium    = [[Stade de Kégué]]
|location   = [[Lomé]], Togo
|attendance =
|referee    = Lenine Dos Santos Rocha ([[Cape Verdean Football Federation|Cape Verde]])
|result     = L
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group B|2026 World Cup qualification]]
|date       = 13 October
|time       = {{UTZ|15:00|2}}
|team1      = {{fb-rt|SSD}}
|score      = 0–0
|report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021041
|team2      = {{fb|TOG}}
|goals1     =
|goals2     =
|stadium    = [[Juba Stadium]]
|location   = [[Juba]], South Sudan
|attendance =
|referee    = Lamin Jammeh ([[Gambia Football Federation|Gambia]])
|result     = D
}}

===2026===
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 27 March
|time = {{UTZ|16:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score = 2–2
|team2 = {{fb|GUI}}
|goals1 = *[[Kevin Denkey|Denkey]] {{goal|26|pen.}}
*[[Idjessi Metsoko|Metsoko]] {{goal|78|pen.}}
|goals2 = *[[Mamady Cissé|M. Cissé]] {{goal|85}}
*[[Serhou Guirassy|Guirassy]] {{goal|89}}
|stadium = [[Moulay El Hassan Stadium]]
|location = [[Rabat]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = [[Amir Loussif]] ([[Tunisian Football Federation|Tunisia]])  
|report = https://www.ftftogo.com/match-amical-international-le-togo-et-la-guinee-se-neutralisent-2-2/
|result = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 31 March
|time = {{UTZ|17:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|NIG}}
|score = 0–1
|team2 = {{fb|TOG}}
|goals1 = 
|goals2 =
*[[Kevin Denkey|Denkey]] {{goal|5}}
|stadium = [[Père Jégo Stadium]]
|location = [[Casablanca]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = [[Mohamed El Baroudi]] ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]]) 
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-03-31/niger-vs-togo/4129717
|result = W
}}
{{football box collapsible
|format     = 1
|date       = 5 June
|time       = {{UTZ|12:00|1}} 
|round      = [[Exhibition match|Friendly]]
|score      = 1–1
|report     = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-05/central-african-republic-vs-togo/4310870
|team1      = {{fb-rt|CTA}}
|goals1     = 
*[[Goduine Koyalipou|Koyalipou]] {{goal|26}}
|team2      = {{fb|TOG}}
|goals2     = 
*[[Kévin Denkey|Denkey]] {{goal|60}}
|stadium = [[Moulay El Hassan Stadium]]
|location = [[Rabat]], Morocco
|attendance = 
|referee    = Mohamed El Baroudi ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]])
|result     = D
}}
{{Football box collapsible
|format     = 1

|round = [[Exhibition game#Association football|Friendly]]
|date = 9 June
|time = {{UTZ|19:00|1}} 
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score = 5–1
|team2 = {{fb|BEN}}
|goals1 = 
*[[Mohamed Tijani|Tijani]] {{goal|39|o.g.}}
*[[Arnaud Komlavi|Komlavi]] {{goal|58}}
*[[Djené]] {{goal|68}}
*[[Kevin Denkey|Denkey]] {{goal|74}}
*[[Kodjo Fo-Doh Laba|Laba]] {{goal|88}}
|goals2 =
*[[Felipe Santos (footballer)|F.Santos]] {{goal|6}}
|stadium = [[El Bachir Stadium]]
|location = [[Mohammedia]], Morocco
|attendance =
|referee = Mohamed El Baroudi ([[Royal Moroccan Football Federation|Morocco]]) 
|report = https://globalsportsarchive.com/en/soccer/match/2026-06-09/togo-vs-benin/4310871
|result = W
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|ZAM}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Togo
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|ZAM}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|TOG}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Zambia
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|ALG}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|TOG}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Algeria
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|ALG}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Togo
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|TOG}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|BDI}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Togo
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|BDI}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|TOG}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Burundi
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}

==Coaches==
[[File:Claude Le Roy, Le360 - Jun 29, 2019.jpg|thumb|100px|[[Claude Le Roy]] became the manager of Togo in 2016]]
:Caretaker managers are listed in ''italics''.
{{Div col|colwidth=22em}}
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Jean Robin (footballer)|Jean Robin]] (1965)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Gottlieb Göller]] (1970–1974, 1984, 1996–1997, 1998–2000)
*{{flagicon|YUG}} [[Boško Antić]] (1987–1989)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} [[Julien Dovi Aguiar]] (1992)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} [[Baboima Ergot]] (1993)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} [[Paul Messan Zougbédé]] (1997)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Eberhard Vogel]] (1997–1998)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} ''[[Kodjovi Mawuéna]]'' (2000)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} [[Bana Tchanile]] (2000–2002)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Diego Garzitto]] (2002)
*{{flagicon|BRA}} [[Antônio Dumas Ramalho Esteves|Antônio Dumas]] (2002–2004)
*{{flagicon|NGR}} [[Stephen Keshi]] (2004–2006, 2007–2008, 2011)
*{{flagicon|GER}} [[Otto Pfister]] (2006)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Henri Stambouli]] (2008)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} [[Kodjovi Mawuéna]] (2008–2009)
*{{flagicon|BEL}} [[Jean Thissen]] (2009)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Hubert Velud]] (2009–2010)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Thierry Froger]] (2010–2011)
*{{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Didier Six]] (2011–2014)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} [[Tchakala Tchanilé]] (2014)
*{{flagicon|BEL}} [[Tom Saintfiet]] (2015–2016)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Claude Le Roy]] (2016–2021)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} ''[[Jonas Komla]]'' (2021)
*{{flagicon|POR}} [[Paulo Duarte (footballer)|Paulo Duarte]] (2021–2024)
*{{flagicon|TOG}} [[Daré Nibombé]] (2024–2025)
*{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Patrice Neveu]] (2026–present)
{{Div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were selected for the [[Exhibition game#Association football|friendly]] matches against [[Guinea national football team|Guinea]] and [[Niger national football team|Niger]] on 27 and 31 March 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.ftftogo.com/fenetre-internationale-fifa-matchs-amicaux-la-liste-des-eperviers-pour-affronter-la-guinee-et-le-niger/|title=Fenêtre internationale FIFA {{!}} Matchs amicaux : La liste des Éperviers pour affronter la Guinée et le Niger|trans-title=FIFA International Window {{!}} Friendly Matches: The Éperviers' roster for the matches against Guinea and Niger|publisher=[[Togolese Football Federation]]|date=19 March 2026|language=fr}}</ref>

''Caps and goals correct as of 31 March 2026, after the match against [[Niger national football team|Niger]].''

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Malcolm Barcola]]|age={{bda|1999|5|14|df=y}}|caps=27|goals=0|club=[[IR Tangier]]|clubnat= MAR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Steven Mensah]]|age={{bda|2003|5|22|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=0|club=[[VfB Oldenburg]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Fabrice Kagbatawouli]]|age={{bda|2002|12|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AC Barracuda|Barracuda]]|clubnat=TOG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Achirafou Yaya]]|age={{bda|2004|7|26|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Academie de Foot Amadou Diallo|AFAD Djékanou]]|clubnat=CIV}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Djené]]|age={{bda|1991|12|31|df=y}}|caps=91|goals=0|club=[[Getafe CF|Getafe]]|clubnat=ESP}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Loïc Bessilé]]|age={{bda|1999|2|19|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[AS Trenčín|Trenčín]]|clubnat=SVK}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Abdoul-Sabourh Bode]]|age={{bda|1995|1|22|df=y}}|caps=17|goals=0|club=[[Stade Malien]]|clubnat=MLI}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Kennedy Boateng (footballer, born 1996)|Kennedy Boateng]]|age={{bda|1996|11|29|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[FC Dinamo București|Dinamo București]]|clubnat=ROM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Steven Nador]]|age={{bda|2002|6|23|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Modena FC 2018|Modena]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Josué Homawoo]]|age={{bda|1997|11|12|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Standard Liège]]|clubnat=BEL}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Izak Akakpo]]|age={{bda|2004|2|28|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[FC Lorient|Lorient]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Pierre Nadjombe]]|age={{bda|2003|5|10|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Alemannia Aachen]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Faad Sana]]|age={{bda|2003|4|15|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[NŠ Mura|Mura]]|clubnat=SVN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Yannis Lawson]]|age={{bda|2004|11|24|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[FC Metz|Metz]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Marvin Senaya]]|age={{bda|2001|1|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AJ Auxerre|Auxerre]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Zuberu Sharani]]|age={{bda|2000|1|7|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[ŠK Slovan Bratislava|Slovan Bratislava]]|clubnat=SVK}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Karim Dermane]]|age={{bda|2003|12|26|df=y}}|caps=27|goals=3|club=[[FC Lorient|Lorient]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Roger Aholou]]|age={{bda|1993|12|30|df=y}}|caps=24|goals=1|club=[[Al-Ittihad SC (Tripoli)|Al-Ittihad]]|clubnat=LBY}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Kodjo Aziangbe]]|age={{bda|2003|12|14|df=y}}|caps=16|goals=0|club=[[Shanghai Port F.C.|Shanghai Port]]|clubnat=CHN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Sadik Fofana]]|age={{bda|2003|5|16|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=1|club=[[US Lecce|Lecce]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Halid Djankpata]]|age={{bda|2005|4|13|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AS Gubbio 1910|Gubbio]]|clubnat=ITA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Kodjo Fo-Doh Laba]]|age={{bda|1992|1|27|df=y}}|caps=56|goals=20|club=[[Al Ain FC|Al Ain]]|clubnat=UAE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Kévin Denkey]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|11|30|df=y}}|caps=46|goals=12|club=[[FC Cincinnati]]|clubnat=USA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mansour Ouro-Tagba]]|age={{bda|2004|12|17|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[VfB Stuttgart II]]|clubnat=GER}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Charles Abi]]|age={{bda|2000|4|12|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Valenciennes FC|Valenciennes]]|clubnat=FRA}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Idjessi Metsoko]]|age={{bda|2002|3|14|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=1|club=[[FC Spartak Trnava|Spartak Trnava]]|clubnat=SVK}}
{{nat fs end}}

===Recent call-ups===
The following players have been called up for Togo in the last twelve months.

{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Abd-Laziz Sambaou]]|age={{bda|1994|4|10|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Inova Sporting Club Association|ISCA]]|clubnat=CIV|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Fadle Tondoro-Cissé]]|age={{bda|1998|6|28|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AS Gbohloé-Su des Lacs|Gbohloé-Su]]|clubnat=TOG|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Youssouf Morou]]|age={{bda|2000|12|31|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Gomido FC|Gomido]]|clubnat=TOG|latest=v. {{fb|SDN}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Mawouna Amevor]]|age={{bda|1991|12|16|df=y}}|caps=15|goals=0|club=[[FC Volendam|Volendam]]|clubnat=NED|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Emmanuel Hackman]]|age={{bda|1995|5|14|df=y}}|caps=11|goals=0|club=[[Turan Tovuz]]|clubnat=AZE|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Kévin Boma]]|age={{bda|2002|11|20|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[G.D. Estoril Praia|Estoril Praia]]|clubnat=POR|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Koffi Holete]]|age={{bda|2003|1|17|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[ASC Kara]]|clubnat=TGO|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Arnaud Komlavi]]|age={{bda|2001|2|10|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[ASC Kara]]|clubnat=TGO|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Amoudane Ouro-Ayeva]]|age={{bda|1997|4|29|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[ASC Kara]]|clubnat=TOG|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Augustin Drakpe]]|age={{bda|2001|12|8|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Slovan Liberec|Slovan Liberec]]|clubnat=CZE|latest=v. {{fb|SDN}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Alaixys Romao]]|age={{bda|1984|1|18|df=y}}|caps=97|goals=0|club=[[Six-Fours Le Brusc FC|Six-Fours Le Brusc]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Yawo Agbagno]]|age={{bda|2000|5|25|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[CS Constantine]]|clubnat=ALG|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Samsondin Ouro]]|age={{bda|2000|3|2|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=0|club=[[FC DAC 1904 Dunajská Streda|Dunajska Streda]]|clubnat=SVK|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Marouf Tchakei]]|age={{bda|1995|12|15|df=y}}|caps=33|goals=3|club=[[Singida Black Stars FC|Singida Black Stars]]|clubnat=TAN|latest=v. {{fb|SDN}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[David Henen]]|age={{birth date and age|1996|4|19|df=y}}|caps=19|goals=1|club=[[SHB Da Nang]]|clubnat=VIE|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ismaïl Ouro-Agoro]]|age={{bda|1996|2|20|df=y}}|caps=16|goals=0|club=[[Tala'ea El Gaish SC|Tala'ea El Gaish]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Yaw Annor]]|age={{bda|1997|12|3|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=2|club=[[National Bank of Egypt SC|National Bank of Egypt]]|clubnat=EGY|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Guillaume Yenoussi]]|age={{bda|1997|6|2|df=y}}|caps=14|goals=0|club=[[Bourges Foot 18]]|clubnat=FRA|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Etienne Amenyido]]|age={{bda|1998|3|1|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[SC Preußen Münster|Preußen Münster]]|clubnat=GER|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Isaac Monglo]]|age={{bda|2007|11|14|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Lyn Fotball|Lyn]]|clubnat=NOR|latest=v. {{fb|SSD}}, 13 October 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Franck Mawuena]]|age={{bda|1992|11|21|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[Tuwaiq Club|Tuwaiq]]|clubnat=KSA|latest=v. {{fb|SDN}}, 9 September 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.<br />
<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.<br />
<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.<br />
<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.<br />
<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.
{{nat fs end}}

==Records==
{{Updated|13 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |last1=Mamrud |first1=Roberto |title=Togo - Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/togo-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=2023-02-02 |archive-date=2023-02-04 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230204162813/https://rsssf.org/miscellaneous/togo-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Togo.''

===Most appearances===
[[File:Alaixromao.jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Alaixys Romao]] is Togo's  most capped player with 97 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1  ||align=left| '''[[Alaixys Romao]]''' || 97 || 0 || 2005–present
|-
| 2 ||align=left| [[Abdoul-Gafar Mamah]] || 93 || 0 || 2000–2016
|-
| 3 ||align=left| '''[[Djené Dakonam]]''' || 89 || 0 || 2012–present
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Emmanuel Adebayor]] || 88 || 32 || 2000–2019
|-
| 5 ||align=left| [[Kossi Agassa]] || 84 || 0 || 1998–2017
|-
| 6 ||align=left| [[Moustapha Salifou]] || 77 || 8 || 2000–2013
|-
| 7 ||align=left| [[Jean-Paul Abalo]] || 74 || 1 || 1992–2008
|-
| 8 ||align=left| [[Komlan Amewou|Komlan Améwou]] || 69 || 5 || 2000–2015
|-
| 9 ||align=left| [[Tadjou Salou]] || 62 || 12 || 1992–2004
|-
| rowspan=2|10 ||align=left| [[Serge Akakpo]] || 61 || 2 || 2008–2017
|-
|align=left| [[Mohamed Kader]] || 61 || 13 || 1995–2009
|-
|}

===Top goalscorers===
[[File:Adebayor.jpg|200px|right|thumb|[[Emmanuel Adebayor]] is Togo's top scorer with 32 goals.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:175px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Emmanuel Adebayor]] ([[List of international goals scored by Emmanuel Adebayor|list]]) || 32 || 88 || {{#expr:32/88 round 2}} || 2000–2019
|-
| 2 ||align=left| '''[[Kodjo Fo-Doh Laba]]''' || 20 || 55 || {{#expr:20/55 round 2}} || 2016–present
|-
| rowspan=2|3 ||align=left| [[Kossi Noutsoudje]] || 13 || 40 || {{#expr:13/40 round 2}} || 1994–2002
|-
|align=left| [[Mohamed Kader]] || 13 || 61 || {{#expr:13/61 round 2}} || 1995–2009
|-
| 5 ||align=left| [[Tadjou Salou]] || 12 || 62 || {{#expr:12/62 round 2}} || 1992–2004
|-
| 6 ||align=left| [[Floyd Ayité]] || 11 || 48 || {{#expr:11/48 round 2}} || 2007–2022
|-
| 7 ||align=left| '''[[Kévin Denkey]]''' || 10 || 42 || {{#expr:10/42 round 2}} || 2018–present
|-
| rowspan=2|8 ||align=left| '''[[Euloge Placca Fessou]]''' || 9 || 36 || {{#expr:9/36 round 2}} || 2012–present
|-
|align=left| [[Adékambi Olufadé]] || 9 || 41 || {{#expr:9/41 round 2}} || 1998–2010
|-
| 10 ||align=left| [[Moustapha Salifou]] || 8 || 77 || {{#expr:8/77 round 2}} || 2000–2013
|-
|}

==Competition records==

===FIFA World Cup===
{{see also|Togo at the FIFA World Cup}}

{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=10|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=39|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
| [[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
| colspan=9 |''Part of {{fb|France}}''
| colspan=6 |''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|-
| {{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
| colspan=9 |''Not a FIFA member''
| colspan=6 |''Not a FIFA member''
|-
| {{flagicon|England}} [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
| colspan=9 rowspan=2 |''Did not enter''
| colspan=6 rowspan=2 |''Did not enter''
|-
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
|-
| {{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
| colspan=9 rowspan=3 |''Did not qualify''
| 2
| 0
| 1
| 1
| 0
| 4
|-
| {{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
| 4
| 1
| 1
| 2
| 3
| 5
|-
| {{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
| 2
| 1
| 0
| 1
| 2
| 2
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
| colspan=9 rowspan=2 |''Withdrew''
| colspan=6 rowspan=2 |''Withdrew''
|-
| {{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|-
| {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
| colspan=9 rowspan=3 |''Did not qualify''
| 5
| 0
| 0
| 5
| 2
| 11
|-
| {{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
| 8
| 2
| 2
| 4
| 9
| 16
|-
| {{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
| 10
| 3
| 4
| 3
| 13
| 13
|-
| {{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
| Group stage
| 30th
| 3
| 0
| 0
| 3
| 1
| 6
|[[2006 FIFA World Cup squads#Togo|Squad]]
| 12
| 8
| 2
| 2
| 22
| 9
|-
| {{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
| colspan=9 rowspan=5 |''Did not qualify''
| 10
| 4
| 2
| 4
| 11
| 10
|-
| {{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
| 8
| 2
| 2
| 4
| 6
| 12
|-
| {{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
| 2
| 0
| 0
| 2
| 0
| 4
|-
| {{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|8||3||3||2||8||7
|-
| {{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States of America}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|10||1||5||4||5||10
|-
| {{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
| colspan=9 rowspan=2 |''To be determined''
| colspan=6 rowspan=2 |''To be determined''
|-
| {{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!{{Tooltip|Group stage|Highest finish}}
!{{Tooltip|1/15|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!3
!0
!0
!3
!1
!6
!—
!81
!25
!22
!34
!81
!103
|}

{{football squad on pitch|align=right
| GK = '''[[Kossi Agassa|Agassa]]'''
| LB = '''[[Ludovic Assemoassa|Amevou]]'''
| RCB = '''[[Jean-Paul Abalo|Abalo (c)]]'''
| LCB = '''[[Daré Nibombé|Nibombé]]'''
| RB = '''[[Massamasso Tchangai|Tchangai]]'''
| DM = '''[[Alaixys Romao|Romao]]'''
| RCM = '''[[Yao Junior Sènaya|Sènaya]]'''
| LCM = '''[[Moustapha Salifou|Salifou]]'''
| LW = '''[[Emmanuel Adebayor|Adebayor]]'''
| RW = '''[[Mohamed Kader|Kader]]'''
| CF = '''[[Chérif Touré Mamam|Touré]]'''
| caption = [[2006 FIFA World Cup]] starting lineup
}}

===Africa Cup of Nations===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=10|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
!rowspan=41|
!colspan=10|Qualification record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!Squad
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Matches played}} 
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}} 
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}} 
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}} 
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}} 
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Cup of Nations|1957]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|colspan=9 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 African Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=9|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|rowspan=2 colspan=9|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|colspan=9|''Did not enter''
|colspan=9|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1968 African Cup of Nations|1968]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|4
|1
|0
|3
|3
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1970 African Cup of Nations|1970]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon|1961}} [[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972]]
|Group stage
|7th
|3
|0
|2
|1
|4
|6
|[[1972 African Cup of Nations squads#Togo|Squad]]
|4
|2
|2
|0
|3
|1
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt|1972}} [[1974 African Nations Cup|1974]]
|colspan=9|'' Withdrew ''
|colspan=9|'' Withdrew ''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1975}} [[1976 African Cup of Nations|1976]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=4|''Did not qualify''
|6
|3
|0
|3
|9
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1978 African Cup of Nations|1978]]
|2
|1
|0
|1
|1
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[1980 African Cup of Nations|1980]]
|4
|3
|0
|1
|3
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|Libya|1977}} [[1982 African Cup of Nations|1982]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|2
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[1984 African Cup of Nations|1984]]
|Group stage
|8th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|1
|7
|[[1984 African Cup of Nations squads#Togo|Squad]]
|8
|8
|0
|0
|11
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[1986 African Cup of Nations|1986]]
|colspan=9 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[1988 African Cup of Nations|1988]]
|2
|0
|1
|1
|1
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Algeria}} [[1990 African Cup of Nations|1990]]
|colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Senegal}} [[1992 African Cup of Nations|1992]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|8
|2
|2
|4
|4
|9
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1994 African Cup of Nations|1994]]
|colspan=9|''Withdrew during qualifying''
|colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[1996 African Cup of Nations|1996]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|8
|1
|3
|4
|5
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|Burkina Faso}} [[1998 African Cup of Nations|1998]]
|rowspan=3|Group stage
|12th
|3
|1
|0
|2
|4
|6
|[[1998 African Cup of Nations squads#Togo|Squad]]
|8
|4
|2
|2
|10
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} {{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[2000 African Cup of Nations|2000]]
|10th
|3
|1
|1
|1
|2
|3
|[[2000 African Cup of Nations squads#Togo|Squad]]
|6
|3
|1
|2
|12
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Mali}} [[2002 African Cup of Nations|2002]]
|12th
|3
|0
|2
|1
|0
|3
|[[2002 African Cup of Nations squads#Togo|Squad]]
|6
|3
|2
|1
|8
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia}} [[2004 African Cup of Nations|2004]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|6
|3
|1
|2
|9
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]
|Group stage
|16th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|2
|7
|[[2006 Africa Cup of Nations squads#Togo|Squad]]
|12
|9
|1
|2
|22
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|6
|3
|0
|3
|9
|7
|-
|{{flagicon|Angola}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]||colspan=9|''Withdrew due to [[Togo national football team attack|rebel attack]]''
|colspan=9|''Withdrew''
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} {{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|8
|1
|3
|4
|6
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|Quarter-finals
|8th
|4
|1
|1
|2
|4
|4
|[[2013 Africa Cup of Nations squads#Togo|Squad]]
|4
|1
|1
|2
|5
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Equatorial Guinea}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|6
|2
|0
|4
|7
|12
|-
|{{flagicon|Gabon}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|Group stage
|16th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|2
|6
|[[2017 Africa Cup of Nations squads#Togo|Squad]]
|6
|3
|2
|1
|11
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Egypt}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|rowspan=4 colspan=9|''Did not qualify''
|6
|1
|2
|3
|4
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Cameroon}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|6
|0
|2
|4
|3
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Ivory Coast}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|6
|2
|2
|2
|8
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|6
|1
|2
|3
|7
|10
|-
|{{flagicon|Kenya}} {{flagicon|Tanzania}} {{flagicon|Uganda}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="9" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
| colspan="9" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2029 Africa Cup of Nations|2029]]
|-
!Total
!Quarter-finals
!8/35
!25
!3
!8
!14
!19
!42
!—
!144
!57
!32
!55
!165
!163
|}

===African Nations Championship===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[African Nations Championship]] record
|-
!colspan=9|Appearances: 1
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[2009 African Nations Championship|2009]]||colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|SUD}} [[2011 African Nations Championship|2011]]
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[2014 African Nations Championship|2014]]
|-
|{{flagicon|RWA}} [[2016 African Nations Championship|2016]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[2018 African Nations Championship|2018]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR}} [[2020 African Nations Championship|2020]]|| |Group stage||11th||3||1||0||2||4|||5
|-
|{{flagicon|ALG}} [[2022 African Nations Championship|2022]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|KEN}} {{flagicon|TAN}} {{flagicon|UGA}} [[2024 African Nations Championship|2024]]
|-
!Total||Group stage ||1/8||3||1||0||2||4|||5
|}

===African Games===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the African Games|African Games]] record
|-
!Year||Round||Pld||W||D||L||GF||GA
|-
|{{flagicon|CGO}} [[Football at the 1991 All-Africa Games|1965]]||Group stage||3||1||1||1||5||9
|-
|{{flagicon|KEN}} [[Football at the 1987 All-Africa Games|1987]]||colspan=7|''Did not qualify''
|-
!'''Total'''||'''1/4'''||'''3'''||'''1'''||'''1'''||'''1'''||'''5'''||'''9'''
|}

==Honours==
===Regional===
* '''[[West African Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (4): [[1982 West African Nations Cup|1982]], [[1983 West African Nations Cup|1983]], [[1984 West African Nations Cup|1984]], [[1986 West African Nations Cup|1986]]
* '''[[WAFU Nations Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': [[2011 WAFU Nations Cup|2011]]
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (1): [[2013 WAFU Nations Cup|2013]]

==References==
{{Reflist}}

==External links==
{{Commons category}}
* [https://www.ftftogo.com/ Togo FA] official site
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/tog/ Togo] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/TOG Togo] at [[FIFA]]

{{Togo national football team}}
{{Navboxes|title=Finalists|list1=
{{2006 FIFA World Cup finalists}}
{{2010 Africa Cup of Nations finalists}}
{{2013 Africa Cup of Nations finalists}}
}}
{{Navboxes|title=Squads and managers|list1=
{{Navboxes
| title       = Togo squads FIFA World Cup
| bg          = #FFCE00
| fg          = #006A4E
| bordercolor = #D21034
| list1       =
{{Togo squad 2006 FIFA World Cup}}
}}
{{Navboxes
| title       = Togo squads Africa Cup of Nations
| bg          = #FFCE00
| fg          = #006A4E
| bordercolor = #D21034
| list1       =
{{Togo squad 1998 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Togo squad 2000 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Togo squad 2002 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Togo squad 2006 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Togo squad 2010 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Togo squad 2013 Africa Cup of Nations}}
{{Togo squad 2017 Africa Cup of Nations}}
}}
{{Togo national football team managers}}
}}
{{Football in Togo}}
{{WAFU Football}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of Togo}}

{{DEFAULTSORT:Togo National Football Team}}
[[Category:Togo national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
